overview: Improve the parts analysis display to be actionable for bike maintenance. Add repair action recommendations and DIY difficulty per part (from Claude), estimated cost ranges (from Claude as fallback when scraping fails), group parts by urgency instead of a flat list, and add an overall maintenance summary card at the top of the results page.
goals: Add repair action and DIY difficulty fields to Claude's structured JSON output
Add estimated cost range to Claude's output as a scraping fallback
Replace flat parts table with urgency-grouped sections
Add a maintenance summary card showing counts and estimated total cost range
nonGoals: No changes to the scraping logic or Performance Bicycle integration
No changes to the interactive image overlay or bounding box features
No backend route changes — all new fields come through the existing POST /api/analyze response
successMetrics: Every part shows a repair action label (Clean & Lube, Adjust, Service, or Replace)
Every part shows an estimated cost range even when scraping returns no results
Parts are grouped into three urgency sections: Action Required, Monitor, Good Shape
Summary card renders at the top of results with correct counts and total cost range
openQuestions: Should Performance Bicycle pricing results still show when available, supplementing the Claude estimate?
Should the summary card link/scroll to the Action Required section on click?

stack.notes: New fields added to Claude Vision prompt and schema; frontend updated to consume them

uiNotes: Summary card: sits above the parts sections, shows 4 stats in a row: urgent count (red), monitor count (yellow), good count (green), estimated total cost range (neutral). Card background is white with a subtle border.
Urgency sections replace the single flat table: 'Action Required' (priority 1-2, red section header), 'Monitor' (priority 3, yellow header), 'Good Shape' (priority 4-5, green header)
Each section is collapsible — open by default, user can collapse 'Good Shape' to focus on problems
Part row updated: add 'Repair Action' badge (Clean & Lube=blue, Adjust=purple, Service=orange, Replace=red) and estimated cost range in the pricing column when no scraping results exist
If scraping DID return results, show those results AND the Claude estimated range below them labeled 'Estimated (if unavailable)'
Empty sections (e.g. no Action Required parts) are hidden entirely — do not show empty section headers
Repair action badge uses a wrench icon: 🔧 or inline SVG

fields: repair_action (new): one of: clean_lube | adjust | service | replace
repair_notes (new): string — specific repair instructions for this part
estimated_cost_min (new): integer — low end USD estimate
estimated_cost_max (new): integer — high end USD estimate

claudeVisionSchema.description: New fields added to each part object in Claude's response

newFields.repair_action: one of: clean_lube | adjust | service | replace
newFields.repair_notes: 1-2 sentence specific repair instruction, e.g. 'Apply leather conditioner and let dry for 24 hours. If cracking is deep, consider a new Brooks B17.'
newFields.estimated_cost_min: integer USD — low end cost to address this part (parts + labor if service/replace, product cost if clean_lube/adjust)

repairActionDefinitions.clean_lube: Part needs cleaning and/or lubrication only — no parts required
repairActionDefinitions.adjust: Part needs adjustment or tuning — no replacement parts required
repairActionDefinitions.service: Part needs a service/overhaul — may involve replacing consumables (cables, housing, bearings)

claudeVisionSchema.costGuidance: Estimate realistic US market cost including labor for service/replace. For clean_lube or adjust, estimate product/tool cost only (typically $5-30). For unknown/vintage parts with no brand, use generic replacement cost.

example.name: Leather Saddle
example.condition_notes: Brown leather saddle showing significant wear, surface appears dry and cracked
example.repair_notes: Apply a quality leather conditioner such as Proofide or Brooks Proofide. If cracking is deep or structural, replace with a new leather or synthetic saddle.

rules: Claude prompt must be updated to include the four new fields: repair_action, repair_notes, estimated_cost_min, estimated_cost_max
repair_action must be one of exactly: clean_lube, adjust, service, replace — backend validates this
estimated_cost_min and estimated_cost_max must be integers ≥ 0
Backend schema validation updated to accept (but not require) the new fields — if missing, defaults: repair_action='service', repair_notes='', estimated_cost_min=0, estimated_cost_max=0
Summary card total cost range = sum of all parts' estimated_cost_min to estimated_cost_max
Urgency grouping: Action Required = priority 1 or 2, Monitor = priority 3, Good Shape = priority 4 or 5
If a section has zero parts, do not render the section header or container
Performance Bicycle scraping results still display when available — Claude cost estimate shown as secondary info

stories.title: Update Claude prompt to return repair action, repair notes, and cost estimate per part
stories.description: As the backend, I want Claude Vision to return repair_action, repair_notes, estimated_cost_min, and estimated_cost_max for each part so the frontend can display actionable repair guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude system prompt updated to request four new fields per part: repair_action (one of: clean_lube, adjust, service, replace), repair_notes (1-2 sentence specific instruction), estimated_cost_min (integer USD), estimated_cost_max (integer USD)
Prompt includes definitions for each repair_action value and cost estimation guidance
Backend schema validation updated: new fields accepted, defaults applied if missing (repair_action='service', repair_notes='', estimated_cost_min=0, estimated_cost_max=0)
POST /api/analyze response now includes the four new fields on each part object
npm test includes a test verifying the new fields are present in the response shape (or defaults applied)
Example: Leather Saddle with fair condition → repair_action: 'service', repair_notes: 'Apply leather conditioner...', estimated_cost_min: 15, estimated_cost_max: 80
Negative case: Claude omits repair_action for a part → backend applies default 'service', does not return 502

stories.title: Replace flat parts table with urgency-grouped sections
stories.description: As a user, I want parts grouped by urgency so the most critical repairs are immediately visible at the top.
acceptanceCriteria: ResultsPage replaces the single flat parts table with three named sections: 'Action Required' (priority 1-2), 'Monitor' (priority 3), 'Good Shape' (priority 4-5)
Each section has a colored header: Action Required=red, Monitor=amber, Good Shape=green
Each section is collapsible — chevron toggle, all sections open by default
Sections with zero parts are not rendered
Within each section, parts are sorted by priority ascending (1 before 2, etc.)
Each part row now includes a 'Repair Action' badge: clean_lube='🔧 Clean & Lube' (blue), adjust='🔧 Adjust' (purple), service='🔧 Service' (orange), replace='🔧 Replace' (red)
Repair notes displayed below condition notes in an italicized smaller font
npm run lint passes
Example: 10-part analysis with 2 priority-1, 5 priority-3, 3 priority-4 → three sections rendered with correct part counts
Negative case: all parts are priority 4-5 → only 'Good Shape' section renders, 'Action Required' and 'Monitor' sections are hidden

stories.title: Show estimated cost range per part in pricing column
stories.description: As a user, I want to see an estimated cost range for each part so I know what to budget even when Performance Bicycle has no results.
acceptanceCriteria: When a part has no Performance Bicycle scraping results, the pricing cell shows the Claude estimated range: 'Est. $15 – $80' in a neutral gray style
When scraping results ARE available, show the scraped results first, then below them show 'Claude estimate: $15 – $80' in a smaller muted style
If estimated_cost_min and estimated_cost_max are both 0, show 'Cost unknown' instead of 'Est. $0 – $0'
Cost range formatted as: 'Est. $[min] – $[max]' with dollar sign and no decimals
npm run lint passes
Example: Leather Saddle with no scraping results → pricing cell shows 'Est. $15 – $80'
Negative case: part with cost_min=0 and cost_max=0 → shows 'Cost unknown', not 'Est. $0 – $0'

stories.title: Maintenance summary card at top of results page
stories.description: As a user, I want a summary card at the top of the results page that shows urgent part counts and total estimated cost so I get an at-a-glance view of my bike's health.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary card renders above the urgency sections, below the bike metadata

Card shows four stats in a row: '⚠ X parts need attention' (red, count of priority 1-2), '● X to monitor' (amber, count of priority 3), '✓ X in good shape' (green, count of priority 4-5), '💰 Est. $min – $max total' (neutral, sum of all parts' cost ranges)
If all parts have cost 0, the cost stat shows 'Cost unknown'
Card has a white background, subtle border, rounded corners, and 16px padding
On mobile (<768px) stats wrap to 2x2 grid instead of a single row
npm run lint passes
Example: 10 parts — 2 urgent, 5 monitor, 3 good, total estimated $120–$340 → card shows all four stats correctly
Negative case: 0 urgent parts → '⚠ 0 parts need attention' is hidden; card only shows monitor + good shape + cost stats
